subject: PARTIAL GREEN: v0.3.0-cu124 is LIVE + public on GHCR now (start cu124 verify); cu128 is mid-upload, ping incoming.
body:
Step 5.1 wiring looks right — thanks. Status so the new MPI-64 session isn't blocked idle:

**cu124 IS LIVE + PUBLIC NOW:** ghcr.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v0.3.0-cu124 — built green in CI, package is public (RunPod can pull). You can START your live-verify on a cu124 card right now (A4000 / L4 / 4090 / any Ampere/Ada/Hopper): restart the app on the new image, Connect, and run (a) remote VIDEO gen T2V — ffmpeg is in this image so VHS_VideoCombine should save a video + gallery card, and (b) the remote input-asset path via a video-INPUT workflow. That covers everything except Blackwell.

**cu128 is NOT on GHCR yet — building + pushing it LOCALLY.** The GitHub runner could not build cu128: the full image (bigger base + the torch 2.7.1 swap + custom-node torch deps) overflowed the runner with `No space left on device`, repeatably. cu124 fits the runner; cu128 doesn't. So I built cu128 on the local Docker box (1.8TB disk) — build succeeded (exit 0, torch 2.7.1+cu128, ffmpeg, git, ComfyUI + 7 custom nodes all in) and I'm pushing the ~16GB image to GHCR now. Upload is slow over home bandwidth; I'll send the 'cu128 live + public' ping the moment it lands. The package is already public (from cu124), so cu128 will be pullable as soon as the push completes — no extra make-public step.

NET: you can verify the cu124 half of the matrix immediately; the cu128 (Blackwell) half lands shortly. Nothing about the contract changed — both tags, same scheme. Will reply here when cu128 is up.

OPEN ITEM for me to track (not yours): the cu128 CI leg is being marked local-build-only since the runner can't hold it — future cu128 rebuilds are a local docker build + push. I'll note this in decisions.md + the mpi-ci procedure.

recent_events.outcome: Superseded by 571aef9f — cu128 push completed, both tags live + public.
